prompt: The subclass constructor fails to compile. What is the bug?
choices: Dog cannot have a no-arg constructor if Animal has a parameterized one
Dog() implicitly calls super() (no-arg), but Animal only defines Animal(String); the compiler error says 'no suitable constructor found'
Animal must declare its constructor protected for Dog to call it
Dog should not extend Animal if Animal has a parameterized constructor
explanation: When a class defines any constructor, the compiler no longer generates a default no-arg constructor. Dog() implicitly inserts super() before its body, but Animal(String) is the only constructor available — there is no Animal(). Fix: explicitly call super("some name") as the first statement in Dog().
